notes: Three tiers of renewable power generation driven by planetary heat, organised around how deep the player is willing to drill. Tier 1 — Geothermal Well 2.5 m (~12 kW): shallow well, ambient subsurface heat, water working fluid, placeable on any planetary surface. Tier 2 — Deep Borehole Geothermal 5 m (~60 kW): Enhanced Geothermal System analogue (hot-dry-rock, 3-5 km bore), still placeable on any planetary surface, still water-driven but at a much higher circulation rate to account for deep-well losses; no biome lock, which is the key win over the Magma Tap for players on non-volcanic worlds. Tier 3 — Magma Tap 5 m (~200 kW): direct lava tap, locked to volcanic biomes, produces obsidian as a byproduct of force-cooling lava against its turbine wall. All three tiers use `fuel.consumesId: "water"` for the Geothermal Well and Deep Borehole (declarative hook against base-game's planned Water fluid — non-operational until VS3 ships); the Magma Tap has no fuel input since lava is environmental. Power numbers are first-pass — `dataGaps` flags the calibration across all three. BuildComponents for the Magma Tap include `composite_plastic` from Chemistry & Plastics for heat-resistant composite panels (only the Magma Tap crosses mod boundaries — the Deep Borehole deliberately stays within base-game materials to reinforce composite-plastic-means-lava-rated).

resources.description: Low-enthalpy geothermal generator. Circulates water as a working fluid through a shallow borehole, extracting ambient subsurface heat to drive a turbine. Placeable on any planetary surface with sufficient crust depth (most biomes qualify; deep-ocean floors do not). Output is modest (~12 kW) but requires only a trickle of make-up water to replace evaporative losses — the steady-state fuel cost is much lower than Coal Power's Combustion Generator. Non-operational until VS3 ships Water as an active fluid; until then the block can be built but will sit idle.

Mid-tier geothermal generator modeled on real-world Enhanced Geothermal Systems (hot-dry-rock / EGS). Drills a 3-5 km bore into deep basement rock, injects water under pressure, and flash-evaporates the returning hot water to drive a turbine. Placeable on any planetary surface — the whole point of drilling deep is that you don't need a volcanic biome; naturally hot rock exists under every crust. Output (~60 kW) lands cleanly between Coal Power's Combustion Generator and the base-game Reactor 7.5 m. Circulation losses at depth are much higher than a shallow well, so water consumption is ~3× the Geothermal Well 2.5 m despite comparable actual evaporation — the fluid cost is nontrivial but the geography is unconstrained. Non-operational until VS3 ships Water as an active fluid.

High-enthalpy geothermal generator that taps lava directly through a refractory bore. Must be placed over a lava pool in a volcanic biome; the game blocks placement elsewhere. Extracts heat by routing lava across a ceramic-and-composite turbine wall — the force-cooled rind flakes off as obsidian, captured as an inventoried byproduct. Output (~200 kW) exceeds the base-game Reactor 7.5 m while consuming no fabricated fuel, but the geographic lock makes it situational rather than universal. Requires composite plastic panels (from Chemistry & Plastics) for the heat-shield layer — the base-game has no material hot-rated for sustained lava contact on its own.
